items.title: Bruce Lehrmann loses legal bid for Brittany Higgins documentary footage
items.summary: Former Liberal staffer Bruce Lehrmann has been knocked back in a bid to gain access to raw documentary footage which featured Brittany Higgins.

items.title: Brother tells of family's 'unimaginable pain' after doctor's death in Tasmania
items.summary: The brother of Royal Hobart Hospital doctor Artyom Avetisyan says the cardiothoracic surgery registrar sounded "extremely exhausted" in the week before his crash death.

items.title: Queensland state electorates of Stretton and Hill to be abolished after review
items.summary: Significant changes were made to 50 of Queensland's 93 electorates by the independent Queensland Redistribution Commission (QRC).

items.title: Alleged Russian spies Kira and Igor Korolev charged with new espionage offence
items.summary: Crown prosecutors presented a new bench charge for the couple of conspiracy to commit espionage.

items.title: Blues coach Laurie Daley to answer critics after State of Origin III against Maroons
items.summary: Laurie Daley says he will address criticism about his performance as Blues coach once the State of Origin series is over.

items.title: Four quick and easy dinners using inexpensive chickpeas
items.summary: From comforting curry to hearty soup, these easy recipes will get chickpeas out of the pantry and onto the menu.

items.title: Broncos winger Josiah Karapani stood down after alleged drink driving, traffic offences
items.summary: In a statement, the club confirmed the 24-year-old had been issued with a notice to appear in court and would not be available for the next NRL game.

items.title: Calls for SA drivers to slow down and pay attention amid spate of fatal crashes
items.summary: Police are calling on drivers to stay attentive and slow down after four people were killed on one region's roads in just 12 days.

items.title: Superannuation switching trend sees billions flow out of retail funds, at a risk
items.summary: There is now more than $1 trillion in self-managed superannuation funds, which means a big chunk of money isn't regulated by APRA, and it's the rate of the shift into SMSFs and less-regulated managed investment schemes that has regulators worried.

items.title: Health minister declines to request coronial inquest into baby Willow Horne's death in Kalgoorlie
items.summary: WA's health minister says she would support a coronial inquest into the death of an eight-month-old girl at Kalgoorlie Health Campus, but says it is a decision for the state coroner.

items.title: Family who spent $250,000 on IVF welcomes new rebate scheme in SA
items.summary: A South Australian couple who spent $250,000 on fertility treatments say the government's IVF rebate will make a "huge difference" for people trying to conceive.

items.title: Outback roads badly damaged by flooding could take year to fix
items.summary: Damage to South Australia's network of outback roads is isolating remote communities, preventing children from getting to school, and slowing the restocking process for pastoralists recovering from drought.

items.title: Father charged with murder and torture of four-month-old baby to remain in custody
items.summary: Police say the baby's condition was consistent with "a child-shaking injury".

items.title: Les Mills, NZ Olympian and founder of international fitness brand, dies aged 91
items.summary: The New Zealand Olympian who opened an Auckland gym in 1968 that grew into an international group fitness brand has died aged 91.

items.title: Winton's film festival kicks off as Red Dog director reveals new thriller
items.summary: Red Dog director Kriv Stenders reveals plans for a new thriller film as the annual Vision Splendid Outback Film Festival kicks off in Winton.

items.title: Serena Williams criticises tennis anti-doping protocol ahead of Wimbledon return
items.summary: As she prepares for her All England Club comeback, Serena Williams claims the tennis anti-doping protocol is "unprofessional" and "unreasonable".

items.title: Canberra man found guilty of attempted murder after stabbing his wife in violent home attack
items.summary: An ACT Supreme Court jury has found a Canberra man guilty of attempted murder after he stabbed his wife during a violent attack in the family's Gungahlin home.

items.title: WA regional courts to reopen a month earlier than planned after security issues
items.summary: Regional West Australian courts closed amid a stoush over security services are set to reopen about a month earlier than originally planned.

items.title: Crows star Chelsea Randall says concussion left with her 'no choice' but to retire from AFLW
items.summary: Adelaide Crows star Chelsea Randall announces her retirement from the AFLW, citing concussion as a major factor in her decision to hang up her boots and step away from the game.

items.title: NASA Swift rescue mission spacecraft will bump telescope back into orbit
items.summary: US space agency NASA is racing to save an aging telescope, used to observe cosmic explosions, from falling back to Earth with a daring rescue mission.

items.title: Artist's impression of NASA's Swift Boost Mission
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Ben Stokes retirement highlights his main character energy was not always a force for good
items.summary: Ben Stokes has been the centre of England's cricketing universe for 15 years. In announcing his retirement mid-match, he highlighted how damaging that main character energy can be.

items.title: World Cup: Has the chaotic, but engaging group stage justified the expanded tournament?
items.summary: Ghana's coach Carlos Queiroz has lashed out at FIFA's expanded 48-team World Cup, despite his team progressing. Is his criticism justified as the tournament moves into the knockout stage?

items.title: Spectacular lightning display at the Eiffel Tower
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: France records 1,000 excess daily deaths as Europe's record heatwave sparks power outages and wildfires
items.summary: More than 150 million Europeans swelter in "extreme heat", which scientists say has been the worst the continent has ever recorded.

items.title: Socceroos to play Brazil at Lang Park in first home game after World Cup
items.summary: The first of the two friendlies will be at Brisbane's Lang Park on September 25, and the second at Townsville on September 29.

items.title: Farmer's push to return lost CSIRO archives reveals 200 years of merino history
items.summary: More than 1,000 kilograms of wool samples, lab notes and technical drawings spent decades gathering dust in a British home. A Victorian sheep farmer just brought them back.

items.title: Maroons recall veteran for Origin decider after Blues' bombshell
items.summary: Billy Slater names Patrick Carrigan in his Queensland squad for State of Origin III despite the Broncos forward having not played since late May due to an ankle injury.

items.title: Mt Rawdon pumped hydro project shelved by Qld government
items.summary: A $6 billion dollar project to rehabilitate a gold mine and turn it into a pumped hydro power station has been dumped by the Queensland government.

items.title: NT Police says new public safety officers on the beat from today will receive the same firearms training as constables
items.summary: The Northern Territory Police Force has moved to reassure Territorians that new, armed public safety officers due to hit the beat from today are fully trained to carry guns.

items.title: Ellyse Perry and Ash Gardner carry Australia to T20 World Cup win over India
items.summary: A powerful partnership by Ellyse Perry and Ash Gardner carries Australia to the greatest run chase in Women's T20 World Cup history, knocking India out of the tournament.

items.title: Tech revival sees ASX close 0.7pc higher, Neuren leaps on EU approval hopes — as it happened
items.summary: The ASX shrugged off a sluggish Wall Street lead to close 0.7 per cent higher as Neuren Pharmaceuticals surged on a potential EU approval for one of its drugs.

items.title: Venezuelan earthquakes rescuers save infant and two boys alive from rubble
items.summary: The three children are among at least 33 people rescued during a weekend multinational operation that continued searching for survivors, while thousands remained unaccounted-for.

items.title: Customers in remote Australia have safety concerns as Telstra phases out CAN Radio phone system
items.summary: Telstra's Customer Access Network Radio has been a lifeline for many for more than 40 years but its technology has reached the end of its life. Customers now fear a new system will not be as reliable in an emergency.

items.title: Man charged with DV murder after woman shot on the Gold Coast
items.summary: Ethan Midgely, 23, faces one domestic violence murder charge after he allegedly shot and killed who police believe was his de facto partner.

items.title: The national program teaching boys as well as girls about endometriosis, periods and pelvic pain
items.summary: Educators behind a national program teaching high school students about periods and pelvic pain hope it will encourage empathy.

items.title: Vanuatu critical infrastructure to remain 'free from militarisation' under Nakamal pact
items.summary: Vanuatu's Prime Minister Jotham Napat and Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese ink the Nakamal Agreement, almost 10 months after Mr Napat pulled out of a planned signing ceremony in Port Vila, citing concerns around sovereignty.

items.title: Lawyers band together as Tasmania's court backlog hits 'crisis point'
items.summary: Routine cases are dragging through Tasmanian courts for years as senior lawyers lament the "crisis" engulfing the state's criminal justice system.

items.title: Coal and gas giants spending tens of millions on programs in schools and children's sport
items.summary: Santos, BHP, Woodside and others have an "extensive presence" in childhood settings through more than 260 programs, a new report identifies, as senators say classroom programs and lessons written by the industry should be examined through a senate inquiry.

items.title: Dozens of Australian Rules players diagnosed with CTE expose scale of AFL's brain injury crisis
items.summary: Australian Rules footballers who never played in the game's premier league are being diagnosed with CTE, revealing that the brain trauma crisis striking all levels of the game.

items.title: As Taylor struggles to handle Hanson, Hastie talks tough on One Nation
items.summary: While Angus Taylor grapples with the Coalition's dilemma of how to keep the centre while diverting voters away from Pauline Hanson's party, Andrew Hastie has been talking tough on One Nation.

items.title: Iran and China are simultaneously driving a major energy transition
items.summary: Two things happened last week that give us a much clearer picture of what the world is going to be like from now on.

items.title: Trump allies in the South are wiping out Black votes
items.summary: States in America's South are scrambling to draw up new electoral maps that would eliminate majority-Black electorates.

items.title: Gulf states 'colder, harder and more transactional' under US-Iran deal
items.summary: Before a flare-up in fighting over the weekend, the interim deal between the United States and Iran was giving the region something it badly needs, analysts say.

items.title: Do you qualify for three hours of free electricity? Here's how it works
items.summary: Under the federal government's Solar Sharer scheme, some Australian households will be able to access three hours of free electricity a day. Here's how you can make the most of it.

items.title: This week in history quiz June 29: Test your smarts on Eminem, Jane Austen and competitive chess
items.summary: This week was a big one in times gone by. From this decade to the 17th century, put your history knowledge to the test.

items.title: Race Around The World recap: Catch the week four films
items.summary: From survival vaults in Norway to a pianist in Pisa, catch up on the films from week four of Race Around The World.

items.title: England captain Ben Stokes to retire from international cricket after third Test vs New Zealand
items.summary: England captain Ben Stokes will retire from international cricket after the third Test against New Zealand, the team has said.

items.title: Australian run of Waitress closing early with Sydney shows cancelled
items.summary: The Australian stage production of Waitress will no longer continue on its national tour, with planned shows in Sydney now cancelled.

items.title: Father of Thai girl found in suitcase shares grief
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Jackson the star as Dockers make it 14 wins in a row
items.summary: The Fremantle Dockers keep rolling along, winning their 14th straight game with a dominant victory over Gold Coast after the Kangaroos beat Essendon. Follow live.

items.title: Face paint, chants and flags — the many ways football fans celebrate the World Cup
items.summary: From donning face paint to viral chants, each fandom has its peculiarities.

items.title: Blues ring in the changes as To'o dumped for decider
items.summary: New South Wales coach Laurie Daley has made five changes to the Blues side for Origin III with Brian To'o missing out for the first time in over five years.

items.title: Young station ringers win Battle of the Barkly at the NT outback's Brunette Races
items.summary: Based on the day in the life of an outback station ringer, the Battle of the Barkly obstacle course has been won by two young cattle industry workers.

items.title: Man charged after four-month-old baby's death in 2024
items.summary: The 22-year-old Bundaberg man has been charged with domestic violence offences of murder and torture.

items.title: New parents struggling with anxiety and depression urged to seek free counselling services
items.summary: New parents often struggle in silence, but a growing number of perinatal clinics offering free mental health support are being rolled out across Australia.

items.title: Treasurer Jim Chalmers promises to 'fix' property tax oversight to protect widows and divorcees
items.summary: The Treasurer is promising to "fix" an unintended consequence of the government's overhaul of property tax breaks to protect widows and divorcees — but he has refused to say exactly how.

items.title: Bali's beauty sinks beneath an 'Island of Trash' as waste issues increase
items.summary: Known to many as the Island of the Gods, residents fear Bali may soon become the "Island of Trash" due to a deluge of tourists, a lack of rubbish education and the partial closure of a much-needed landfill facility.

items.title: Love story: Holding onto the glimmers of my dad, Blues legend Steve Mortimer
items.summary: Erin Mortimer's dad, Bulldogs and Blues legend Steve Mortimer, was diagnosed with dementia at just 60 after years of concussions during his playing career. Erin is holding on to the special memories, and the glimmers of him that remain.

items.title: Thai teenager's family grieves as Australian charged with murder says death was 'out of my control'
items.summary: The family of a 17-year-old girl in Thailand speak of their grief as an Australian man is charged with her murder.

items.title: Beyond the Shed training program prepares WA shearers for their future and a stronger local workforce
items.summary: Almost 100 shearing shed workers across regional WA sign up to a new training program aimed at helping contractors and their teams find work beyond the shearing industry.

items.title: Long-awaited Perth-to-Bunbury train service reopens
items.summary: The disruption-plagued Perth-to-Bunbury train service is back on track following years of breakdowns and delays, but locals remain frustrated over the rollout.

items.title: Wallabies and All Blacks to play Bledisloe Cup Tests on Anzac Day at Lang Park
items.summary: For the first time in 123 years, the Wallabies and All Blacks will play a Bledisloe Cup Test on Anzac Day, with Lang Park hosting in 2027, 2029 and 2031.

items.title: Curtin University program helps connect young autistic people to cyber security industry
items.summary: The cybersecurity sector is projected to need tens of thousands of roles filled by 2030. Now, a university-based program is helping provide those workers by tapping into the abilities of autistic people.

items.title: NSW shark-spotting drone program expanded after Coogee Beach shark attack
items.summary: Shark-spotting drones will provide year-round coverage at about 70 beaches along the NSW coast as part of an expanded surveillance program announced after a Sydney mother was attacked by a suspected white shark.
